task_completion.description: The Drawio file contains two pages named 'Service Architecture' and 'Service Inventory'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Drawio application interface, look at the page tabs at the bottom of the canvas. There should be tabs labeled 'Service Architecture' and 'Service Inventory'.

task_completion.description: The Service Architecture page visualizes all required components inside a Kubernetes Namespace boundary
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Service Architecture' page, there should be a large container shape (representing the Kubernetes Namespace) enclosing at least 13 shapes (11 microservices, Redis, and Ingress/Gateway).

task_completion.description: The components in the architecture diagram are connected by lines labeled with protocols
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines (edges) connecting the shapes in the architecture diagram, with text labels on or near the lines explicitly stating 'gRPC' or 'HTTP'.

task_completion.description: The Service Inventory page contains a table listing each service's name, language, and port
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Service Inventory' page, there should be a table structure with columns for Service Name, Language, and Port, populated with data for the microservices.

task_completion.description: The Service Architecture page is exported as a PNG file to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'boutique_architecture.png' is visible on the Desktop, in a file manager, or open in an image viewer.

integrity.description: The agent used the Drawio GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: The Drawio application interface should be visible in the screenshots, showing the diagram being actively edited or viewed, rather than just a script generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent opened the boutique_services.yaml file to read the dependencies and details
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'boutique_services.yaml' file should be seen open in a text editor (like VS Code, Mousepad) or in the terminal (using cat, less, etc.) in at least one screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The architecture diagram must contain 13 shapes (11 services: frontend, cartservice, productcatalogservice, currencyservice, paymentservice, shippingservice, emailservice, checkoutservice, recommendationservice, adservice, loadgenerator; plus redis-cart and ingress-gateway). Connections should be labeled with protocols (mostly gRPC, some HTTP). The inventory table must list these services with their languages (Go, Node.js, Python, C#, Java) and ports (e.g., frontend:8080, cartservice:7070).
